title: AI Trust & Safety Digest
tagline: A curated current snapshot of AI trust, safety, and governance, including as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
lead: Today’s signal is concentrated in evaluation and assurance: new work probes hidden failure modes in therapeutic dialogue, fairness auditing in clinical ML, hallucination detection in multimodal systems, and limits of formal AI safety verification. Alongside that, Google highlights a behavioral-dispositions alignment evaluation frame, pointing to continued movement from broad capability testing toward more targeted assessments of model behavior and risk.

items.title: Persona-based counseling attack exposes hidden LLM vulnerabilities in mental health settings
items.why_it_matters: It surfaces high-stakes failure modes where empathetic dialogue can reinforce harmful beliefs over multiple turns.

items.title: Google outlines evaluations for alignment of behavioral dispositions in LLMs
items.why_it_matters: It advances measurement of model tendencies and character-like behaviors beyond task accuracy alone.

items.title: FairLogue introduces intersectional fairness analysis for clinical machine learning models
items.why_it_matters: It helps auditors detect compounded disparities that single-axis fairness checks can miss in healthcare.

items.title: Fine-grained token grounding proposed as a stronger detector of LVLM hallucinations
items.why_it_matters: It targets a persistent multimodal reliability problem with more precise grounding-based detection.

items.title: Paper argues AI safety verification is intrinsically incomplete under Kolmogorov complexity limits
items.why_it_matters: It suggests some safety guarantees may be fundamentally unattainable, shaping verification expectations.

items.title: Framework proposed for evaluating adaptive AI-enabled medical devices over iterative updates
items.why_it_matters: It addresses how to assess safety and performance when medical AI systems and datasets keep changing.

items.title: Symbolic property analysis aims to improve assurance for DRL agents in systems and networking
items.why_it_matters: It supports safer deployment by reasoning about agent behavior across operating conditions.

items.title: OpenAI launches pilot Safety Fellowship for independent alignment and safety research
items.why_it_matters: It may expand external safety research capacity, though it is a program announcement rather than a technical result.

note: Selected from recent trust-and-safety relevant posts and papers by labs, evaluators, standards bodies, policy organizations, and arXiv, then summarized with GPT-5.4. Includes as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
